business.headline: ED arrests 2 in Rs 183-crore online ponzi scam; accused returned from DubaiThe case was registered in December 2024 in Thane, accusing Sudhir Kotadiya, Umang Kotadiya, Jaysukh Sakhariya and others of running a fraudulent digital investment scheme that allegedly collected large sums from investors in Gujarat and Maharashtra.

Live: LPG crisis caused by BJP govt's disastrous policy, says Karnataka ministerLPG Crisis Live Updates: Amid reports of commercial LPG cylinder shortage, Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge slammed the Centre, calling the crisis a result of the BJP government's "disastrous policy" and questioned PM Narendra Modi's foreign policy moves. Meanwhile, the shortage is beginning to affect services on the ground as well—food for the public at the "Didi Ki Rasoi" run by Jeevika didis at Rohtas Sadar Hospital has been stopped due to lack of LPG cylinders, and patient meals could also be affected if supply is not restored.

Which four shipping routes control global trade? Karan Adani breaks it downKaran Adani explained that a large part of global shipping passes through four critical choke points. These routes act as key gateways for international trade and energy transport.

How is the global energy landscape changing? Karan Adani explainsSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Karan Adani said the world has witnessed repeated disruptions in supply chains over the past few years, forcing countries to rethink how they manage energy and trade.

After Air India, IndiGo imposes fuel surcharge amid Middle East crisisThe energy crisis triggered by the ongoing war in the Middle East is all set to make your air travel costlier. India's biggest domestic airline, IndiGo, on Friday announced it will impose Rs 425 to Rs 2,300 fuel surcharge on domestic and international flights from Friday (March 14).

Energy, ports and materials define Adani Group's strategy: Karan AdaniKaran Adani emphasised that the group's business expansion is not random but guided by long-term economic changes. Energy transition, digital infrastructure and industrial materials are areas expected to grow significantly in the coming years.

Adani Ports ready to handle energy cargo amid global disruptions: Karan AdaniSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Karan Adani, Managing Director of Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd said supply chains worldwide have faced repeated shocks in recent years, forcing countries to rethink their dependence on highly globalised trade networks.

In an AI-driven world, storytelling and creativity will matter most: Christina WallaceWallace spoke about the growing impact of artificial intelligence. While AI will change many jobs, she believes human creativity will remain essential.

Storytelling will be the most valuable skill in the AI era: Christina WallaceOne skill that Wallace believes will become even more important in the age of AI is storytelling. While machines can produce data and structured information, they struggle to capture human emotion and experiences.

IndiGo, Air India seek tax relief as airspace bans hit operations, raise costIndiGo and Air India have urged the government to cut aviation fuel taxes and reduce private airport charges as the Iran conflict and Pakistan airspace ban force longer routes, raising operational costs and straining airline finances.
India's bond market must expand to finance Viksit Bharat goals, says Anurag MittalIndia will require massive investment to sustain growth, says Mittal.

India needs stronger bond market to fund Viksit Bharat vision, says Anurag MittalMittal explained that while India's growth story has been strong, future development will need long-term capital from a wider range of investors, not just government spending.

LPG production up by 30%, avoid panic buying: Govt amid shortage fearsAddressing concerns over reports of shortages in some areas, Sujatha Sharma, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, said LPG production has been increased by around 30% since March 5 to meet rising demand.

What's driving gold's rally? World Gold Council's Sachin Jain explainsSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Sachin Jain, Regional CEO (India) at the World Gold Council, said the factors supporting gold prices remain strong.

Will gold's rally continue? WGC's Sachin Jain explains what lies aheadSpeaking at a session during the India Today Conclave 2026, central bank buying, strong investment demand and global uncertainty continue to support gold prices, says Sachin Jain, Regional CEO (India) at the World Gold Council.
Sensex closes 1,471 points lower, Nifty below 23,200; L&T down 7%The S&P BSE Sensex tanked 1,470.50 points to close at 74,563.92, while the NSE Nifty50 plunged 488.05 points to end at 23,151.10.

Why can't India produce more of its own oil and gas? Anil Agarwal flags policy hurdlesSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Agarwal said the country has the resources and talent to produce more energy at home.

Are foreign powers blocking India's energy self-reliance? Anil Agarwal answersVedanta chairman Anil Agarwal said India has the resources to produce more energy domestically but policy changes and faster decision-making are needed to unlock the sector.

Could rising gas prices make your quick commerce orders costlier?Quick commerce has become the go-to option for instant groceries and food deliveries. But with LPG prices rising and supply concerns growing, the cost pressure may be building behind the scenes. Could this soon make your quick commerce orders costlier? Let's have a look.

March 15 Advance Tax deadline: Who should pay now to avoid extra interestThe March 15 deadline for the final advance tax payment is around the corner. Individuals earning from business, rent, freelancing or investments may have to settle dues to avoid interest charges. But who exactly should pay?

entertainment.headline: Bhumi Pednekar praises Dhurandhar, wants to work with Aditya Dhar, Ranveer Singh Bhumi Pednekar shared that watching Dhurandhar made her eager to collaborate with filmmaker Aditya Dhar and actor Ranveer Singh in the future.

How Netflix's One Piece live action is good, but not quite the same as the anime Netflix's One Piece live action stays faithful to the story, but condensing 40 to 50 anime episodes into an eight-episode season makes the vast, quirky world feel different from its anime counterpart.
Ram Gopal Varma confirms Sarkar 4, film to go on floors soon Ram Gopal Varma confirmed that he will begin shooting Sarkar 4 next month, marking the return of his acclaimed political crime franchise.

You can't fake it: Saurabh Shukla on filmmaking with integrity Saurabh Shukla reflected on his journey as a filmmaker, saying he makes films primarily as a way to express himself and share the world he believes in.

Wanted: Bollywood hits for the family audience Traditionally, the family drama has been the strongest crowdpuller at the Hindi box office. Lately, however, the family audience is left with few options for a weekend's outing together, with films riding heavy violence and gore taking over the box office. Film experts weigh in on the trend.

Anil Kapoor still earns from 2008 film Slumdog Millionaire. Know how much Actor Anil Kapoor revealed he still receives royalties for his role in the 2008 film Slumdog Millionaire. The actor shared the anecdote during his session at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi.

It's my loss: Anil Kapoor reveals why he turned down Dhurandhar 2 cameo Anil Kapoor recently revealed that he was approached by Aditya Dhar for a cameo in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. The actor shared his reasons behind declining the offer.

Anil Kapoor reveals the real secret behind his stubble and trademark moustache At India Today Conclave Delhi 2026, actor Anil Kapoor had a witty secret to reveal. The actor said his stubble and moustache played a decisive role in his early career.

Film school said no, Bollywood embraced him: Anil Kapoor talks cinema, fitness At the India Today Conclave 2026, Anil Kapoor rejected comparisons with Dev Anand and spoke about carving his own identity in Bollywood. He revealed how his signature stubble helped shape his on-screen image.

Telugu actor Rajendra Prasad apologises for MGR remark, calls it a mistake Veteran Telugu actor Rajendra Prasad issued a video apology after his comments about legendary Tamil actor and former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MG Ramachandran aka MGR sparked criticism. His speech received condemnation from Tamil actors.

Iron Lung review: Mark Fischbach's claustrophobic sci-fi horror thrives on dreadRelease Date:Fri Mar 13, 2026 Set after a cosmic catastrophe, Iron Lung follows a lone prisoner sent to explore a blood ocean. Headlined by Mark Fischbach, the film is based on the indie horror game by David Szymanski.

Still Shining first impression: Jinyoung, Kim Min-ju lead a tender healing romanceRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Park Jin-young and Kim Min-ju's Still Shining is a slow-burn romance about companionship and growing up. The drama, which is now streaming on Netflix, blends nostalgia, quiet emotions and tender performances.

Music chose me: Viral sensation Paramjeet Kaur on her singing journey Viral Punjabi singer Paramjeet Kaur talks about her unexpected music journey, viral fame and comparisons with Sidhu Moosewala at the India Today Conclave.

Swara Bhaskar's mother Ira slams Dhurandhar for violent portrayal of Muslims, Pakistan Ira Bhaskar, mother of actor Swara Bhaskar, criticised Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar for its violent and stereotypical portrayal of Muslims, highlighting concerns over ideological bias in contemporary Indian cinema.

Santhosh Narayanan-Arivu row: 5-year beef resurfaces over Enjoy Enjaami rights The long-standing conflict between music composer Santhosh Narayanan and rapper Arivu regarding the global hit Enjoy Enjaami has resurfaced, with both artists making renewed claims over credit, compensation, and creative ownership.

Cocktail 2 teaser to release with Dhurandhar 2 in theatres, first-look poster out The teaser of Cocktail 2, starring Rashmika Mandanna, Shahid Kapoor, and Kriti Sanon, will release in theatres with Dhurandhar: The Revenge. The makers announced the film's release date along with first-look posters.

Oscars 2026 adds last-minute opera-ballet act amid Timothee Chalamet row: Report Misty Copeland will perform at the 2026 Oscars shortly after responding to Timothee Chalamet's viral comments about ballet and opera. The timing has sparked widespread discussion.

Don't use Jailer, Coolie songs in promos: Makers take Chennai Super Kings to court Makers of Jailer, Jailer 2 and Coolie filed a copyright infringement suit against Chennai Super Kings for using music and dialogues from their films. The case was heard at the Madras High Court on March 13.

Anurag Dobhal's manager shares his first photo after car crash, gives health update YouTuber Anurag Dobhal, also known as UK07 Rider, is out of the ICU after a car crash that he livestreamed for his followers. His manager has now shared a fresh update on his recovery.

general news.headline: Begging the world, including India, to buy Russian oil: Iran hits out at USIran's foreign minister accused the United States of double standards on Russian oil, saying Washington once pressured India to cut imports but is now urging countries to buy Russian crude.

Kharg: Iran's 'Forbidden Island' where Trump may explore putting boots on the groundA small island, less than the size of Lakshadweep, was bombed by the US on Friday in what Donald Trump has called the heaviest bombing raid in the region in history. Is this stage one of an eventual strategy to place American boots on the ground and carry out a ground invasion?

Why LPG, not petrol or diesel, took the hit amid Hormuz chokeA week after the US-Israel strikes on Iran started the war in the Middle East, long queues for LPG cylinders began appearing across India. The Centre maintained that there was no shortage but an LPG crunch is evident. Why are petrol and diesel supplies not affected, given they also come from the same region?

For 'friend' India, Iran clears passage of 2 LPG tankers through HormuzIran's Ambassador to India, Mohammad Fathali, signalled that Indian vessels could receive safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. He described India as a friend and pointed to shared interests between the two countries amid the Middle East war.

Anil Kapoor still earns from 2008 film Slumdog Millionaire. Know how muchActor Anil Kapoor revealed he still receives royalties for his role in the 2008 film Slumdog Millionaire. The actor shared the anecdote during his session at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi.

US bombs Iran's Kharg Island, most powerful raid in Middle East history: TrumpKharg Island is Iran's main oil export hub and a critical energy lifeline. Any attack there could disrupt global oil supplies, as much of Iran's crude exports pass through the island.

Either I collapse or get up and go: Anil Agarwal reflects on loss of his sonVedanta chairman Anil Agarwal said the death of his son Agnivesh has strengthened his resolve to continue working for India, urging opportunities for youth and calling for world-class education at home.

What's the fuss? IPL-linked teams have used Pak players even with shadow banSunrisers Leeds ensured that the first-ever Hundred auction was a memorable one as they swooped in to get Pakistan's Abrar Ahmed. Amidst all the talk of the shadow ban, they are not the first team with IPL roots to use Pakistan players.

Wanted: Bollywood hits for the family audienceTraditionally, the family drama has been the strongest crowdpuller at the Hindi box office. Lately, however, the family audience is left with few options for a weekend's outing together, with films riding heavy violence and gore taking over the box office. Film experts weigh in on the trend.

Iran threatens strikes on US-supported oil facilities after Kharg Island bombedIranian armed forces threatened to launch retaliatory strikes on oil facilities operated by companies cooperating with the US in the Middle East, after President Donald Trump said American forces had destroyed military targets on the strategic Kharg Island.

Meta to cut 20% of workforce amid AI push, over 15,000 jobs at riskMeta is reportedly planning to lay off around 20 per cent of its global workforce, potentially impacting over 15,000 employees. It is believed that the Mark Zuckerberg-led firm may take this step following plans for massive spending on AI. Here are the details.

Live: Missile hits helipad inside US Embassy in Baghdad amid high security alertUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: As the war enters its 15th day, tensions continue to rise across the Middle East. The United States is deploying additional forces, including elements of a Marine Expeditionary Unit and an amphibious assault ship, though officials say it does not necessarily signal an imminent ground operation. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth claimed Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is wounded. Meanwhile, Iran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz have disrupted global oil flows. Fighting has also intensified in Lebanon, where Israeli strikes have killed 773 people, including more than 100 children.

The T20 World Cup of comebacksIf the 2024 T20 World Cup win came as a huge relief, the 2026 victory feels relatable. From Sanju Samson, to Ishan Kishan to Rinku Singh, each player fought their own personal battles en route to create history for India.

Drunk man urinates on woman from auto, mob beats him to death in ChennaiA 30-year-old man in Chennai died after a mob beat him for allegedly urinating on a woman from an autorickshaw. Police have registered a case and are reviewing CCTV footage to identify the suspects.

US sends 2,500 Marines, warship to Middle East as Iran conflict intensifiesThe move comes as fighting escalates across the Middle East, with Iran launching missile attacks, Israel striking targets, the Strait of Hormuz disrupted, and oil prices climbing amid fears of wider conflict.

Ujjain man arrested for stealing neighbour's undergarments over one-sided loveA 25-year-old man in Ujjain was arrested after CCTV footage showed him stealing a married neighbour's undergarments. Police said he confessed to the act, claiming he was obsessed with her.

Social media put Himachal's Raulane festival on the map, but not without drawbacksLast year, photos and videos from the Raulane festival left the internet mesmerised. This year, many travellers made their way to Himachal Pradesh to witness the spectacle for themselves.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

He might be helping them: Trump claims Putin could be aiding Iran in warTrump said he believes Vladimir Putin may be helping Iran in its war with the United States and Israel, while Russia denies sharing intelligence with Tehran as the conflict escalates.

Is Yogi rewriting his political brand, and with it, his legacy?Over the years, Yogi has cultivated a highly recognisable political identity. Labels such as "Bulldozer Baba" and "encounter CM" became shorthand for a governance model that emphasised visible demonstrations of law-and-order enforcement — demolitions of properties linked to alleged criminals, high-profile police encounters, and uncompromising rhetoric around crime and security.

The 996 question: What China's tech rise means for IndiaChina did not emerge as a tech giant overnight. The country invested heavily over decades, and a relatively tougher work culture has become a subject of discussion in many countries of the Global South. The tech revolution that gained momentum alongside the 996 work culture, often linked to a long tradition of diligence, raises an important question: can such a model work in India's favour? The question comes at a time when many are raising concerns about the rise of hustle-driven work culture in India.

El Nino is coming: Why India may sweat like never before in 2026According to new data from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts, the planet may experience a strong or even "super" El Niño event.

Trump administration to get $10 billion fee from investors in TikTok US deal: WSJInvestors including Oracle and Silver Lake are making payments to the US Treasury as part of an agreement transferring control of TikTok's American operations from ByteDance to a new US-led venture.

Live-in for 4 years, dumped me for another woman: Woman blames cop, kills herselfA woman in Agra died by suicide after posting a video accusing a police constable of exploiting and harassing her during a four-year live-in relationship. The constable was suspended, before he was arrested and sent to jail.

sports.headline: Pakistan Cricket Board plans legal action after Muzarabani skips PSL to join IPL Blessing Muzarabani faces legal action from the PCB after leaving his PSL contract with Islamabad United to join Kolkata Knight Riders in the IPL. This is the second time in as many years that a player has ditched PSL for the IPL.

What's the fuss? IPL-linked teams have used Pak players even with shadow ban Sunrisers Leeds ensured that the first-ever Hundred auction was a memorable one as they swooped in to get Pakistan's Abrar Ahmed. Amidst all the talk of the shadow ban, they are not the first team with IPL roots to use Pakistan players.

Saudi, Bahrain Grand Prix races to be cancelled amid Middle East conflict Formula 1 is set to cancel the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix in April amid escalating Middle East conflict, with security concerns and logistics forcing the decision.

Would've gone for sportsman spirit: Salman Agha on controversial run-out vs BAN Pakistan batter Salman Ali Agha said that he would have chosen sportsmanship after his controversial run-out during the second ODI against Bangladesh on March 12. Agha was dismissed by Mehidy Hasan Miraz and it led to an on-field spat between the batter and the Bangladesh players.

The T20 World Cup of comebacks If the 2024 T20 World Cup win came as a huge relief, the 2026 victory feels relatable. From Sanju Samson, to Ishan Kishan to Rinku Singh, each player fought their own personal battles en route to create history for India.

Maaz Sadaqat's all-round show helps Pakistan level series with 128-run win Pakistan hammered Bangladesh by 128 runs in the second ODI of the three-match series on Friday, March 13 at Shere Bangla National Stadium, Dhaka to draw level in the rubber. Maaz Sadaqat's all-round brilliance in just his second ODI helped his team make a comeback in the series.

Indian women's team qualifies for Hockey World Cup 2026 The Indian women's hockey team qualified for the Hockey World Cup 2026. The qualification came as a process of the team reaching the final of the FIH Hockey World Cup Qualifiers.

Kuldeep, Vanshika shine in grand wedding ceremony, Chahal steals the show India spinner Kuldeep Yadav and his fiancée Vanshika Singh's pre-wedding festivities in Mussoorie have been gaining traction on social media, as his former teammate Yuzvendra Chahal is seen enjoying the celebrations to the fullest.

Muzarabani ditches PSL for IPL, replaces Mustafizur Rahman Zimbabwe's Blessing Muzarabani has joined Kolkata Knight Riders as a late replacement for Mustafizur Rahman. The 6 ft 8" pacer was the joint second highest wicket-taker in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Harman-Smriti era sees sharp rise in Indian women's participation in cricket The golden era of Indian women's cricket team under Harmanpreet Kaur and Smriti Mandhana has seen massive ripple effects. According to a study, participation in women's cricket in India has doubled across 14 states since 2020.

BAN vs PAK: Agha left fuming over bizarre run out, fans add insult to injury Pakistan batter Salman Agha got run out by Bangladesh captain Mehidy Hasan Miraz while trying to pick up the ball at the non-striker's end. The dismissal has once again ignited the debate over the spirit of cricket.

Kushal Das, former Indian football general secretary, dies at 66Former AIFF general secretary Kushal Das, who played a key role in bringing the 2017 FIFA U-17 World Cup to India and strengthening the country's football administration, passed away in New Delhi at the age of 66.

Indian football mourns Ravinder Singh, Namdhari FC star who died at age of 25Young footballer Ravinder Singh, contracted to Namdhari FC, died at 25 after collapsing during a tournament in Punjab. The Indian Football League and clubs across the country paid tribute, with a minute's silence planned as a mark of respect.

Premier League: Arsenal face Martinelli dilemma as summer transfer battles loomArsenal weigh up life without Gabriel Martinelli, Real Madrid keep tabs on Premier League midfielders, and Nottingham Forest fight for survival in a turbulent season. A look at the biggest talking points shaping the league this weekend.

Five storylines as Formula 1 heads to Shanghai for its first sprint weekendFormula 1 heads to Shanghai for the Chinese Grand Prix, the first Sprint weekend of the 2026 season. From Kimi Antonelli's battle with George Russell to Lewis Hamilton returning to one of his favourite circuits, here are five key storylines to watch.

BCCI has no say in Sunrisers Leeds buying Pakistan's Abrar Ahmed: Rajeev ShuklaBCCI vice-president Rajeev Shukla reacts after Sunrisers Leeds sign Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed in The Hundred auction, saying the board has no role in overseas leagues. Sunrisers Leeds grabbed the headlines with their big-money move for the Pakistan spinner during the auction on March 12.

CSK incomplete without Dhoni, but IPL 2026 might be his last season: PathanIrfan Pathan feels that MS Dhoni may play his last IPL season this year as he gets ready for his 19th campaign in the tournament. Pathan feels that CSK as a franchise will be incomplete without Dhoni and tipped him to play a big role this season.

Sunrisers Leeds justify Pak spinner Abrar Ahmed signing: Very pleased to get himSunrisers Leeds coach Daniel Vettori defended the signing of Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed after the Hundred auction. The move triggered online backlash from Indian fans, with some even calling for a boycott of Sunrisers Hyderabad.

Watch: CSK record signing Prashant Veer learns Tamil from sister before IPL 2026IPL 2026: CSK's Rs 14.20 crore signing Prashant Veer is already winning hearts before IPL 2026 begins. A viral video shows the young all-rounder learning Tamil from his sister as he prepares to join the Chennai Super Kings camp.

The Hundred: Tariq, Abrar only Pak players picked as teams set for 2026 seasonThe first-ever men's and women's Hundred auctions came to an end with all the attention on the Pakistan players. Abrar Ahmed and Usman Tariq were the only Pakistani players picked during the auction.

Trophy is home: Suryakumar thanks Team India fans after T20 World Cup winT20 World Cup: Suryakumar Yadav sent a heartfelt message to the Indian cricket team fans after the T20 World Cup 2026 win. Suryakumar dedicated the win to the supporters and thanked them for their part in the journey to the title.

World Cup-winning captain under fire: The strange scrutiny of Suryakumar YadavDespite leading India to a successful title defence at the ICC T20 World Cup 2026, Suryakumar Yadav has found himself at the centre of surprising criticism. A section of fans continues to question his place as skipper, overlooking his key contributions with the bat and bold leadership decisions.

Suit, stocks and squash: Ramit Tandon now eyes the Olympic dream for IndiaSwapping the world of finance for the Squash court, Ramit Tandon now eyes the Olympic dream for India. In an exclusive with India Today, the World No.38 talks about his journey from the cubicle to chasing his love for the sport, major goals in 2026 and more.

England lifts media ban on Ashes stars despite ongoing performance reviewECB has lifted the temporary restriction on centrally contracted England players speaking to the media. The move comes amid scrutiny following England's Ashes defeat and a series of off-field controversies involving players during recent tours.

Sunrisers Leeds X account suspended after backlash over Abrar Ahmed Hundred signingHours after making the signing of Abrar Ahmed during the Hundred auction, Sunrisers Leeds' X account was suspended on Thursday, March 12. The Leeds team is fully owned by the Sun Group, who are the owners of Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL.

South Africa the stupidest team of T20 WC, could've knocked out India: VaughanT20 World Cup: Michael Vaughan labelled South Africa the stupidest team during the recently-concluded T20 World Cup 2026. Vaughan said that South Africa should have lost their Super 8 clash against the West Indies to put India on the brink of elimination during the second phase of the tournament.

Duplantis breaks pole vault world record for 15th time at Uppsala World AthleticsArmand 'Mondo' Duplantis soared to another historic milestone, breaking the pole vault world record for the 15th time. The Swedish superstar cleared 6.31m at the Mondo Classic in Uppsala.

MS Dhoni needs to bat higher than No.8 for CSK in IPL 2026: PujaraCheteshwar Pujara feels MS Dhoni should bat higher for CSK in IPL 2026. The former India batter believes the Chennai Super Kings legend can still change matches if he gets more time at the crease.

Trump says Iran should skip FIFA World Cup for their safety, lifeIran's sports minister said on Wednesday that it was not possible for his nation's athletes to participate after the US launched airstrikes alongside Israel against Tehran. The attacks triggered a region-wide conflict that has shown no signs of abating.

Indian Wells: Djokovic admits bitter feeling after losing pre-quarters to DraperWorld No. 3 Novak Djokovic lost to defending champion Jack Draper in a fiercely-fought Round of 16 match that lasted 2 hours and 37 minutes. Djokovic won the opening set, but he went down 4-6, 6-4, 7-6(5) in California on Thursday.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
